description:
Extend the plugin/participant config with per-event policy, layered using bl-2148's state-branch -> local -> per-invocation inheritance.

## Schema sketch (settled by bl-26de SPEC)

PluginEntry gains an optional events block:

  events:
    claim:  { required: false }
    review: { required: false, gate: false }
    close:  { required: false, gate: false }
    sync:   { required: false }

Each event's policy is independently configurable; missing entries inherit from the layer above; the top-level layer defaults reproduce today's behavior.

## Legacy field handling

- sync_on_change remains readable; serde alias semantics map it to a coarse default for the events block when no events block is present.
- enabled remains the master switch.
- Documented precedence: per-invocation flag > local repo > state-branch > legacy sync_on_change > implicit default.

## Tests

- Round-trip: legacy config (only enabled + sync_on_change + config_file) deserializes to the same effective policy as today.
- Layered override: state-branch sets required=true; local layer overrides to required=false; effective policy is local.
- Per-invocation flag (--no-sync, --sync) shadows both.
title: Config inheritance: per-event participant policy
